LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Interaction.prompt: Write a direct social interaction between pawns from the POV pawn's side of the moment.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Interaction.enhancement: Keep the reaction tied to the supplied social text; do not add unstated history or dialogue.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MentalState.prompt: Write a mental-state event as immediate pressure on thought, impulse, and action.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MentalState.enhancement: Stay inside the named break/state and observed behavior; do not diagnose beyond supplied facts.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Tale.prompt: Write a notable non-social RimWorld tale as something the pawn lived through or remembers.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Tale.enhancement: Use the tale facts as the event source; do not turn the entry into ordinary social chatter.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MoodEvent.prompt: Write a colony-wide mood event through how it touches this pawn.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_MoodEvent.enhancement: Show felt effect rather than explaining game mechanics; keep the condition's scope clear.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Thought.prompt: Write a temporary thought or mood memory as inner pressure, relief, or irritation.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Thought.enhancement: Treat the thought as internal context, not as a new external event unless the facts say so.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Inspiration.prompt: Write inspiration as sudden useful clarity catching in the pawn's mind.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Inspiration.enhancement: Focus on what the inspiration makes the pawn want to do next; do not invent success.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Romance.prompt: Write a relationship milestone as an emotional change between the two people.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Romance.enhancement: Center the changed bond itself; do not backfill a conversation or private scene.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Work.prompt: Write sampled colony work as a concrete moment in the pawn's labor.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Work.enhancement: Keep routine work modest and physical; let passion, strain, or dread come only from supplied fields.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Hediff.prompt: Write a health-condition event through the pawn's body and mood.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Hediff.enhancement: Let the condition press on the entry without inventing treatment, diagnosis, or recovery.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Raid.prompt: Write a raid or hostile arrival from the pawn's immediate experience of danger.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Raid.enhancement: Keep it grounded in threat, noise, orders, fear, and survival; do not invent battle outcomes.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Quest.prompt: Write a quest lifecycle event as promise, resolution, or loss.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Quest.enhancement: Use the lifecycle signal and supplied quest facts; do not invent rewards, blame, or consequences.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_DayReflection.prompt: Write an end-of-day reflection that weighs the day as a whole.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_DayReflection.enhanceme: Choose the supplied moments that still matter tonight instead of listing every event.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Arrival.prompt: Write a factual colony-arrival note about how this pawn joined or began here.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Arrival.enhancement: Use only scenario and arrival facts; do not invent backstory, welcome scenes, or future plans.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Death.prompt: Write a factual death note about how the colonist died.
LanguageData.DiaryEventPrompt_Death.enhancement: Use only supplied cause, body-part, weapon, condition, and setting facts; keep it brief.
